chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT HYDERABAD Criminal Bail Application No.D-39 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) 1. For orders on M.A.No.1636/2025. 2. For orders on office objections. 3. For orders on M.A.No.1637/2025. 4. For hearing of main case. 11.03.2025 Mr. Ghulam Murtaza Shaikh, Advocate for applicants along-with applicants. == 1. Urgency is granted. 2. Overruled. 3. Exemption application is allowed subject to all exceptions. 4. Through instant Criminal Bail Application, applicants Muhammad Hussain, Azhar Ali, Sajid Ali Shaikh, Arslan Khan and Irfan Ahmed Shaikh seek protective bail in FIR No.47 of 2025 registered under sections 394, 452, 147, 149, 506/2, 395, 427, 148, 114 PPC, 6K, 7 ATA at PS B-Section Dadu. It is inter alia contended by learned counsel for the applicants that the applicants have been falsely implicated in this case by the complainant; that the applicants intend to surrender before the concerned Court for appropriate relief but they apprehend their imminent arrest as the premises of the Trial Court has been cordoned off by the police, therefore, he prays that the applicants may be admitted to protective bail and operation of NBW if any issued by Trial Court against them may be suspended. Be that as it may, without touching merits of the case, applicants are admitted to protective bail for a period of five days from today subject to furnishing their solvent surety in the sum of Rs.20,000/- (Rupees Twenty Thousand) each and P.R bonds in like amount to the satisfaction of Additional Registrar of this Court. This order shall cease to have its effect on expiry of five days from today i.e. 15.03.2025 or on which date the applicants surrender themselves before the concerned Court whichever occurs earlier as the case may; in case of failure, the bail bonds executed by the applicants shall stand forfeited automatically.

Be that as it may, without touching merits of the case, applicants are admitted to protective bail for a period of five days from today subject to furnishing their solvent surety in the sum of Rs.20,000/- (Rupees Twenty Thousand) each and P.R bonds in like amount to the satisfaction of Additional Registrar of this Court. This order shall cease to have its effect on expiry of five days from today i.e. 15.03.2025 or on which date the applicants surrender themselves before the concerned Court whichever occurs earlier as the case may; in case of failure, the bail bonds executed by the applicants shall stand forfeited automatically. The operation of NBW if any, issued against the applicants by the learned Trial Court is hereby suspended during the said period. Criminal Bail Application stands disposed of in above terms. JUDGE JUDGE Muhammad Danish*
